text: In pylint, find the refactoring-checker visitor method that fires the `use-implicit-booleaness-not-len` warning when `len()` is used inside a boolean test condition such as `if len(my_list):`. The method visits Call nodes, walks past any enclosing boolean operations, and must avoid crashing when `len()` is invoked with no arguments at all (e.g. `if len():`).

fivegram_audit.ngram: .Call) -> None: # a len(S) call is used
a len(S) call is used inside
